material.mattext:
Session 3 Quiz – Functions and Scope

Test your understanding of function declarations, expressions, arrow functions, and scope.

Topics Covered: - Function declarations vs expressions - Arrow functions - Parameters and return values - Function scope - Global vs local scope - Closures

Instructions: - 16 questions from the Session 3 question bank - 20 minute time limit - 3 attempts allowed - 16 points total (1 point per question)

After syncing the question bank to Canvas:
Which correctly creates an array of three numbers?
What does `.length` return for this array? ```js const items = ["a", "b", "c", "d"]; console.log(items.length); ```
Which method adds an item to the END of an array?
What will this print? ```js const tasks = ["email", "report"]; tasks.push("meeting"); console.log(tasks.length); ```
Removes and returns the last element
Removes and returns the first element
Adds an item to the end
Returns the last element without removing it
What is the index of "CSS" in this array? ```js const topics = ["HTML", "CSS", "JavaScript"]; ```
How do you access the LAST element of any array?
What will this print? ```js const scores = [85, 92, 78]; scores.push(100); console.log(scores[3]); ```
Which of these correctly creates an object?
How do you access a property called `name` in an object called `student`?
What will this print? ```js const book = {title: "Web Dev", pages: 300}; console.log(book.title); ```
Can you modify a property of an object declared with `const`?
Yes, you can change the property values
No, const objects are completely immutable
Only if you add new properties
What will this code print? ```js const user = { name: "Jordan", loginCount: 5 }; user.loginCount = 6; console.log(user.loginCount); ```
When you have related properties that belong together
Objects and variables are the same
What does this create? ```js const account = { type: "Checking", balance: 500 }; ```
How many properties does this object have? ```js const car = { brand: "Toyota", model: "Camry", year: 2024, color: "blue" }; ```
What will this print? ```js const product = { name: "Keyboard", price: 79.99 }; console.log(product.price); ```
Which is NOT a valid way to access an object property?
Both a and b are valid
Can you add a new property to an object?
No, all properties must be defined when you create it
Yes, you can add properties anytime
Only if it's declared with let
What will this print? ```js const order = { items: 3, total: 150.00 }; console.log(order["total"]); ```
Which term describes the `name` in this object? ```js const person = {name: "Alex"}; ```
What will this code output? ```js const settings = { brightness: 80, volume: 50 }; settings.brightness = 100; console.log(settings.brightness); ```
